label: Weight stigma present (1) vs absent (0)
definition: Overall decision for whether the post contains weight-stigmatising content.
include_if: Any explicit or implied demeaning, blaming, shaming, stereotyping, or moral judgement about body weight.
exclude_if: Neutral, supportive, or clinical discussion without demeaning language.
example: 'People are overweight because they are lazy.'

label: Explicit blame
definition: Directly attributes higher body weight to personal moral failure or laziness.
include_if: Statements that assign fault to individuals for body size.
example: 'No excuses, weight gain is your own fault.'

label: Moralising food/body language
definition: Frames foods or bodies as morally good/bad, clean/dirty, worthy/unworthy.
include_if: Good/bad person framing based on eating or body size.
exclude_if: Practical nutrition advice without moral labels.
example: 'Cheat meals make you weak and disgusting.'

label: Appearance-focused judgement
definition: Judges personal worth or social value based on body appearance.
include_if: Ridicule, disgust, or social devaluation tied to body shape.
exclude_if: Neutral reference to appearance in a non-judgemental context.
example: 'Beach bodies are for disciplined people only.'

label: Stereotyping larger bodies
definition: Uses generalisations about people in larger bodies as a group.
include_if: Claims that a group is lazy, undisciplined, unhealthy, etc.
exclude_if: Post-specific behaviour without broad group assumptions.

label: Healthism / implied personal failure
definition: Implies health status is fully controlled by individual choices and willpower.
include_if: Ignores social, medical, economic, or environmental determinants of health.
example: 'If you are sick, you just didn't try hard enough.'

label: Neutral/supportive/non-stigmatising
definition: Content that is supportive, neutral, or non-judgemental.
example: 'Health goals should be tailored and respectful to each person.'
